assertions.description: INCORRECT: The KSZ8567 7-port Ethernet switch uses differential net names TX1+, TX1-, RX1+, RX1-, TX2+, TX2-, RX2+, RX2-, TX3+, TX3-, RX3+, RX3-, TX4+, TX4-, RX4+, RX4-, TX5+, TX5-, RX5+, RX5-. All 20 of these are reported in bus_analysis.uart as UART signal candidates solely because of the TX/RX prefix. These are 100BaseT Ethernet differential-pair port signals, not UART. The UART detector should exclude nets that are Ethernet differential pairs (e.g., if they also appear as differential_pairs entries with a non-CAN type).

INCORRECT: bus_analysis.i2c reports {net: 'AVDDH', line: 'SDA', devices: 11x IC1}. AVDDH is a KSZ8567 analog supply rail (Analog VDD High), confirmed by the many bypass capacitors (C52-C59) on this net and the AVDDL/AVDDH power rail naming convention. The false match occurs because IC1 pin 98 is named 'SDI/SDA/MDIO' — a multiplexed name containing the substring 'SDA'. The I2C detector is matching on pin-name substring rather than net-name semantics, producing a spurious I2C bus with 11 devices and no pull-ups.
